description: Internal data contradiction between table and text
input_text:
2. Data

Our dataset covers 500 publicly listed firms on the Shanghai Stock Exchange from 2015 to 2020, yielding a balanced panel of 3,000 firm-year observations. After applying our sample restrictions (excluding financial firms, firms with missing key variables, and firms delisted during the period), our final analysis sample consists of 350 observations.

3. Results

Table 2: Main Regression Results

Variable          | (1) OLS    | (2) FE     | (3) IV-2SLS
ESG_Score         | 0.023***   | 0.018**    | 0.041**
                  | (0.006)    | (0.008)    | (0.019)
Firm_Size         | 0.156***   | 0.089**    | 0.167***
                  | (0.023)    | (0.041)    | (0.052)
Leverage          | -0.034*    | -0.012     | -0.045*
                  | (0.019)    | (0.022)    | (0.027)
Firm FE           | No         | Yes        | Yes
Year FE           | Yes        | Yes        | Yes
N                 | 500        | 500        | 485
R²                | 0.234      | 0.412      | --
First-stage F     | --         | --         | 18.7

As shown in Table 2, our full sample of 500 firm-year observations yields consistent results. The ESG coefficient is positive and significant across all specifications. Column (3) uses industry peer ESG scores as an instrument, with a first-stage F-statistic of 18.7 exceeding the Stock-Yogo critical value.
rationale: The text states the final sample is 350 observations after restrictions, but Table 2 reports N=500 (and N=485 for IV). These numbers are irreconcilable. Either the sample description or the table is wrong. This is a fundamental data integrity issue that undermines credibility.
